name: MS Teams AI Agent Tab App with MCP Gateway Integration
description:
Design and develop a Microsoft Teams **Tab-based application** embedding an **AI Agent** that reasons using **AWS Bedrock LLMs** and orchestrates tool execution via **Topcoder MCP Gateway**. The application will support SSO, and a responsive tab UI with friendly End User capabilities.

---

## Goals

* Build a production-ready Microsoft Teams **Tabs App** with conversational AI capabilities.
* Integrate **AWS Bedrock** for LLM inference and **Topcoder MCP Gateway** for tool orchestration.
* Allow **Users** to interact with tools through a friendly UI powered by AI reasoning.

---

## Features

### 1. Microsoft Teams Tab App

* Built using **React + Fluent UI**
* Installed in Teams and rendered as a tab
* [SSO via **Azure AD / Entra ID**](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoftteams/platform/tabs/how-to/authentication/tab-sso-overview)
   * When the user opens the Teams tab, Microsoft Teams injects an authentication token.
   * The frontend sends this token to the backend with each request.
   * The backend validates this token to ensure the user belongs to the same Microsoft 365 tenant / organization.
   * Only validated users from the tenant are allowed to interact with the agent and invoke tools.
* Tenant-aware installation
* [Microsoft Teams Tab App Documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoftteams/platform/tabs/what-are-tabs)

### 2. AI Agent (LLM via AWS Bedrock)

* Uses **LangChain JS** with `@langchain/community/llms/bedrock`
* Supports:
  * Prompt orchestration
  * Conversation memory
  * Streaming responses
  * Retry/fallback for throttling and timeouts
* [AWS Bedrock Overview](https://aws.amazon.com/bedrock/)
* [LangChain JS](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchainjs)
* [LangChain Python MCP Adapter (Optional)](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain-mcp-adaptersor)

### 3. MCP Gateway Integration

* Connects securely to **Topcoder MCP Gateway**

  * Provided in forums: sample Python code for how to connect to the MCP tools.
* Agent can:

  * Select tools based on prompt
  * Call tools and return structured responses
* Typed schemas for tools
* Environment-aware (dev/test/prod) - make it configurable
* No secrets in code (all in Key Vault / Secrets Manager)
* [Topcoder MCP Endpoint](https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v6/mcp/mcp)

### 4. End User UX

* User logs in via SSO
* Main interface provides:

  * Prompt input (chat-like)
  * AI-generated summaries
  * Tool execution results (Adaptive cards/cards/tables)
  * Feedback/status indicators (loading, retry, success/failure)

### 5. Expected User Experience and Interactions

The app should deliver an intuitive and efficient experience for non-technical users to interact with backend tools through AI.

#### a. Login & Role-based Access

* Users land on the tab and are automatically signed in via SSO
* The app identifies the user and displays corresponding UI

#### b. Prompt Interaction

* A text input box lets the user type a request (e.g., "Get current project status")
* Upon submission, the agent parses and responds
* The UI shows AI status/progress indicator during processing, (e.g. Thinking, Calling XX tool, processing yyy .. etc)
*  The user should not be allowed to send new response until AI finishes or a stop button should be used (like ChatGPT)

#### c. Streaming Response

* For longer responses, content streams token-by-token (ChatGPT-like experience)
* UI updates in real-time as the model responds

#### d. Tool Invocation Flow

* The LLM selects and invokes an MCP tool behind the scenes
* UI may show:

  * "Invoking Tool: ToolName"
  * Result section with structured content (table, adaptive cards, or text)
* If parameters are missing, the bot prompts the user for them

#### e. Error Handling & Retry

* If a tool fails or times out, the agent retries or gracefully shows an error message
* Common user-friendly errors: "Tool unavailable", "Missing input", "Try again later"

#### f. History / Memory

* (Optional) Session memory retained per user so they can ask follow-up questions
* Example: "What about last week?"

#### g. Chat History Side Panel

* Users will have access to a side panel showing their chat history
* History will display:

  * Prompt entered
  * AI responses
  * Tool names invoked (if any)
  * Timestamps
* Users can click a past interaction to view the full exchange in the main panel
* The panel should support filters (e.g., Today, This Week) and optional search by keyword or tool name

---

## Technology Stack

* **UI:** React.js + Fluent UI (Teams UI)
* **Auth:** Azure AD SSO
* **Agent:** Python [LangChain MCP Adapter](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain-mcp-adaptersor) OR Node.js [LangChain JS](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchainjs) + AWS Bedrock
* **Tool Invocation:** Topcoder MCP Gateway ([API](https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v6/mcp/mcp))
* **Secrets Management:** Azure Key Vault ([Docs](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/key-vault/general/overview)) or AWS Secrets Manager ([Docs](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/secretsmanager/latest/userguide/intro.html))
* **Hosting:** AWS (container or static site + backend)

---

## Deliverables

* Source code for:

  * Teams Tab App UI
  * Agent logic (Bedrock + MCP integration)
* Teams manifest
* Documentation:

  * Architecture & sequence diagrams
  * Security and IAM configuration
  * Deployment instructions
  * User Guide
* Demo video:
  * App installation
  * SSO flow
  * User flow with 2 tool executions

---

## References & Resources

* [Microsoft Teams Tab App Dev](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoftteams/platform/tabs/what-are-tabs)
* [Bot Framework SDK for Node.js](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/javascript/api/botbuilder)
* [Azure AD Authentication for Teams Apps](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoftteams/platform/tabs/how-to/authentication/auth-aad-sso)
* [LangChain JS](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchainjs)
* [LangChain Python + MCP](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain-mcp-adaptersor)
* [AWS Bedrock](https://aws.amazon.com/bedrock/)
* [Topcoder MCP Gateway API](https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v6/mcp/mcp)
* [Azure Key Vault](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/key-vault/general/overview)
* [AWS Secrets Manager](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/secretsmanager/latest/userguide/intro.html)

---

## Acceptance Criteria

* App installs as a tab in Teams and authenticates using Azure AD
* Users can enter prompts and receive streaming AI responses
* At least 2 external MCP tools are integrated and callable via the agent
* Secrets are securely stored and never hardcoded
* Deployment is reproducible manually via documented steps

name: Topcoder Identity Service fixing Roles Endpoints Defects
description:
## Project Overview
The Identity Service project is used  to validate username / password logins, lookuap roles, etc...  It's currently implemented in Java and targets MySQL/Informix.
We want to revise the project to use **TypeScript** and point to **Postgres** via **Prisma**, but **keep the functionalities untouched, such as redis cache, Kafka event bus, etc**.  

In previous challenges: [Topcoder Identity Service: revise using Typescript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/6cf9672f-6a55-4043-8661-df9917bb3271),  [Topcoder Identity Service: updating User Resources with TypeScript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/6ab232e5-16c4-4e81-a909-827ac897bbdf) , [Topcoder Identity Service: updating GroupResource with TypeScript and Postgres [Repost]](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/b1fd57aa-5616-4c0e-8b08-683c1b1981fb) and [Topcoder Identity Service: updating AuthorizationResource with TypeScript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/a4f17d0f-2196-4d00-8c29-3a33871da825), we'v setup the project structure with TypeScript and Postgres, and migrated **RoleResource**, **UserResource**, **GroupResource** and **AuthorizationResource**.
In this f2f challenge, we're going to fix the Roles Endpoints defects in the new code.

## Detail Requirements

Compared with v3 java code, the following are missing for roles endpoints:
1, get roles endpoints, should return specified fields according to selector parameter, see https://github.com/topcoder-platform/identity-api-v6/blob/6e352321d2d78fb975f212bc30aee1225f70397a/src/api/user/user.controller.ts#L524 as an example;
2, assign role, and deassign role should call dao.checkRole as in the java code, see https://github.com/appirio-tech/identity-service/blob/bf2428ea6997e115b317eb419c5e2c1c6029cdf5/src/main/java/com/appirio/tech/core/service/identity/resource/RoleResource.java#L429;
3, hasRole endpoint, the logic should call dao.checkRole;
4, We should return role id from assigning or deassign role endpoints;
5, user.service.ts#addUserToDefaultGroups should be implemented.

## Technology Stack

- NodeJS
- Java
- MySQL
- Postgres version **16.3**
- ORM: Prisma (with PostgreSQL)

## Resources
- Exist Identity Service:  https://github.com/appirio-tech/identity-service/tree/dev
- Revised Identity Service: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/identity-api-v6/tree/develop
- Data in MySQL&Postgres: Will provide in forum
- Dependencies for Exist Identity Service: Will provide in forum

## Submission Guidelines

Your submission should include:
- A patch file against the develop branch of Revised Identity Service latest commit
- A validation markdown file that covers how you verify the functionalities

## Final Notes

- Ask questions in the challenge forum for clarifications, Do not make assumptions without approval.
- Maintain clean, structured, and well-documented code .

name: AI Aided Asset Versioning Platform User Panel Frontend Challenge
description:
### **Challenge Overview**

Marketing teams spend significant time manually adapting creative assets to different channels (Instagram, LinkedIn, YouTube, etc.) and formats (Story, Feed, Carousel, etc.). This challenge aims to define the system for a platform that uses AI to automate asset resizing, adaptation, and export.

The system will support two components:
* **Admin Panel** for managing platforms, formats, and AI behavior
* **User Panel** for uploading assets and generating adapted variants

Your task in this challenge is to create the react.js frontend for the user panel and integrate with the provided backend apis. 
### **Tech Stack**
* Python 3
* Postgres
* React.js

---

### **Functional Scope**

Design: https://marvelapp.com/prototype/11a2f99e/screen/97582714
- Signup is not in scope
- Forgot password is not in scope

The backend codebase is provided in the forum, and you are responsible to integrate the user panel with apis too. 
For detailed UI to api mapping, please check the document in the forum. 

#### Login Screen
* User can login with username and password, after login, the user can be redirected to admin or user panel based upon user's role.  (No need to have two separate login screen like design)

#### User Panel

* **Upload Workflow**

  * Upload high-resolution assets (e.g., PNG, JPEG)
  * Validate format and size constraints

* **Asset Adaptation**

  * Select desired platforms and formats
  * Edit mode - Manual edit (user can type the text and select the logo etc.). Note that the manual editing is handled in the frontend, and after applying changes, you need to save the metadata and the updated image to the backend. 
  * Preview variant outputs
  * Trigger batch generation of all selected assets

* **Download & Delivery**

  * Download generated files individually or in bulk (e.g., ZIP)
  * Track status of asset generation jobs

On this page: https://marvelapp.com/prototype/11a2f99e/screen/97686410, please add a Select AI Provider dropdown before the "Generate With AI" button. 

### **Deliverables**
- zip file containing the source code
- readme with deployment guide and verification steps

name: Phase 1: AI Agent for Innovation: From Idea to Live Challenge
description:
This is Phase 1 of a 3-part program to build an **AI Copilot Agent** that helps users define and launch structured innovation or development challenges on platforms like **Topcoder, HeroX, Kaggle, Zindi**, or internal company systems.

Your mission is to implement the **reasoning and guidance engine** of the Copilot — the backend AI logic that interacts with a user to understand their goal, assess its feasibility for a challenge, clarify details, and ultimately construct a well-scoped, structured spec ready for submission.

The AI agent must **ask dynamic, adaptive questions**, guide scoping with reasoning, and refine its behavior based on user answers and platform requirements. No hardcoded scripts — the system should **reason, recall, and respond contextually**.

---

## Objectives

Your Copilot must be able to:

1. Accept a high-level user input (e.g. “I want to build an app for cleaning services”)
2. Initiate a **scoping dialogue**:
   * Ask contextual questions about project status
   * Clarify whether this is design, development, testing, or PoC
   * Suggest possible scoping directions
   * Let the user confirm or reject them
   * Reassess scope feasibility iteratively
3. Once scope is confirmed, **load a dynamic schema** (platform-specific challenge fields)
4. Drive structured Q\&A to complete the spec fields
5. Retrieve similar challenges via RAG to inform questions and structure
6. Output a **structured, reasoned spec** (JSON or similar)
7. Store and adapt to user feedback per section or suggestion

---

## How Scoping Should Work

The Copilot is not allowed to impose a scope.

Instead, it should:

1. **Evaluate scope feasibility**
   Example prompt:
   > "This seems like a broad goal. Can you tell me what part is currently your priority, or what’s already done?"
2. **Ask project clarifying questions**
   * “What stage is the project in?”
   * “Do you have mockups or designs?”
   * “Is this the first task, or part of a series of challenges?”
3. **Propose possible scoped ideas** based on the user's reply, e.g.:
   > “Based on what you shared, we might start with designing the booking screen. Does that work for you?”
   * If user says yes → continue
   * If user says no → ask what they prefer, then re-evaluate if that fits challenge boundaries (timeline, cost, deliverable size)
4. **Repeat the cycle** until both agent and user agree on a scoped unit of work
5. **Then start compiling the spec** with dynamic field-driven questions

---

## Use of Agent Frameworks

You must use an existing AI agent orchestration framework:

Recommended:
* **LangGraph**
* **CrewAI**
* **AutoGen**
* **SuperAgent**
* **Flowise**

You may also use **OpenAI Assistants API** if it includes:
* Memory across turns
* Function calling
* Tool-based interaction

---

## Dynamic Field Schema (Configurable)

Challenge requirements vary per platform. Your Copilot must:

* Load challenge field definitions dynamically (JSON, YAML, or API)
* Use them to:
  * Ask relevant questions
  * Track checklist progress
  * Format the final output

Example (suggested, not required):

```json
{
  "platform": "Topcoder",
  "challenge_type": "Design",
  "fields": {
    "title": { "required": true },
    "overview": { "required": true },
    "objectives": { "required": true },
    "tech_stack": { "required": false },
    "timeline": { "required": true },
    "prize_structure": { "required": true },
    "judging_criteria": { "required": true }
  }
}
```

Fields must not be hardcoded. Make the schema **configurable per platform and challenge type**.

---

## RAG Integration

Integrate a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline:
* Use a vector database (e.g., FAISS, Pinecone, Qdrant)
* Store past challenge specs, templates, and suggestions
* Retrieve relevant examples based on user topic, category, or phase
* Use these as grounding for:
  * Question phrasing
  * Estimating timeline
  * Suggesting judging criteria
  * Platform-specific formatting

---

## Feedback Loop Handling

As users answer questions or reject suggestions:
* Capture their feedback (e.g., “React won’t work for us”)
* Adapt future prompts during the session to avoid repetition
* Log reasoning traces for all suggestions
* If rejected → ask why → re-suggest

Example:

```json
{
  "field": "tech_stack",
  "ai_suggestion": "React",
  "accepted": false,
  "reason": "We prefer Flutter for cross-platform",
  "prompt_update": true
}
```

---

## Output Format (Structured Spec + Reasoning)

Final spec output should include:
* Structured challenge fields (e.g., JSON or object)
* Reasoning per field (why it was filled this way)
* Confidence level (optional)

Example:

```json
{
  "title": "Cleaner Booking Flow - UI Prototype",
  "overview": "Design 3 screens for customer booking in a cleaning app",
  "tech_stack": ["Figma"],
  "timeline": { "submission_days": 6 },
  "reasoning_trace": [
    {
      "field": "tech_stack",
      "source": "User confirmed mockup design only; no code needed",
      "confidence": 0.92
    }
  ]
}
```

---

## Tech Stack
* **LLM**: OpenAI GPT-4 (with Functions or Assistants API)
* **Agent Framework**: LangGraph (preferred) or CrewAI
* **Prompt Routing & Memory**: LangChain (optional if using LangGraph)
* **RAG Framework**: LangChain or LlamaIndex
* **Vector Store**: Qdrant (preferred), Pinecone, or FAISS (local dev)
* **Embeddings**: OpenAI text-embedding-3-small or Instructor-XL
* **Server**: FastAPI (Python) or Node.js + Express

---

## Deliverables

1. Agent backend with:
   * Scoping dialogue logic
   * Schema-aware Q\&A logic
   * RAG integration
   * Reasoning trace generation
2. 3 end-to-end session logs (user → Copilot → spec)
3. README explaining:
   * Agent orchestration setup
   * Memory handling
   * Prompt strategies
   * Schema configurability

---

## Bonus Points
You’ll earn bonus points in functionality and UI evaluation criterias for:

name: Topcoder Review API - Design Prisma Schema & OpenAPI Spec for Submissions API Migration
description:
This series of challenges focus on migrating the existing Submissions API functionality into the Topcoder Review API. The goal is to ensure all Submissions API endpoints are available in the new Topcoder Review API with identical request/response structures to maintain backward compatibility with existing services that depend on the Submissions API.

This challenge focuses on designing the database schema (using Prisma ORM) and OpenAPI documentation (via NestJS) for the migrated Submission API.

### Background

The current Submissions API handles several key functions including:

- Submission management (create, read, update, delete)
- Review management
- Review types management
- Review summations

As per the requirements, we need to integrate these functions into the new Review API while maintaining the same endpoint structure and behavior to avoid updating dependent services.

### Technology Stack

- **Backend**: NestJS
- **ORM**: Prisma with PostgreSQL
- **ID Format**: nanoID (to maintain compatibility with the new Review API)
- **API Documentation**: OpenAPI/Swagger
- **Package Manager**: pnpm

### Required Links

- GitHub Repo: [https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api)
- Prisma Schema: [https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api/blob/develop/prisma/schema.prisma](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api/blob/develop/prisma/schema.prisma)

### Detailed Requirements

#### 1. Prisma Schema Extensions

Extend the existing Prisma schema to include models for the Submissions API:

**Important: Avoid duplication with existing models in the Review API.** The Review API already has some overlapping models and functionality, particularly with reviews. You should:

1. **Identify overlapping models** - The Review API already contains a **review** model with similar functionality to the Submissions API's Review model.
2. **Extend existing models** - Where models overlap, extend the existing Review API models rather than creating new ones.
3. **Create new models** - Only create new models for functionality that doesn't exist in the Review API.
4. **Maintain compatibility** - Ensure that the existing Review API functionality continues to work while adding Submissions API compatibility.

Models to evaluate for integration:

- **Review** - The Review API already has a **review** model. Extend this model to support the Submissions API review functionality rather than creating a new model.
- **Submission** - Create a new model if not already present in the Review API.
- **ReviewType** - Evaluate if this can be integrated with the existing ScorecardType enum or if a new model is needed.
- **ReviewSummation** - Create a new model if not already present in the Review API.

### 2. API Endpoints

Implement the following endpoints with identical request/response structures as the existing Submissions API. 

**For endpoints that overlap with existing Review API functionality, create adapter layers that maintain the original Submissions API interface while using the Review API's underlying functionality.**

#### Submission Endpoints

- **POST /submissions** - Create a new submission
- **GET /submissions** - List submissions with filtering
- **GET /submissions/:submissionId** - Get a specific submission
- **PUT /submissions/:submissionId** - Update a submission
- **PATCH /submissions/:submissionId** - Partially update a submission
- **DELETE /submissions/:submissionId** - Delete a submission
- **GET /submissions/:submissionId/download** - Download submission file
- **POST /submissions/:submissionId/artifacts** - Add artifact to submission
- **GET /submissions/:submissionId/artifacts** - List artifacts for a submission
- **DELETE /submissions/:submissionId/artifacts/:file** - Delete an artifact
- **GET /submissions/:submissionId/artifacts/:file/download** - Download an artifact
- **GET /submissions/:challengeId/count** - Count submissions for a challenge

#### Review Endpoints

- **POST /reviews** - Create a new review (adapt to use existing Review API functionality)
- **GET /reviews** - List reviews with filtering (adapt to use existing Review API functionality)
- **GET /reviews/:reviewId** - Get a specific review (adapt to use existing Review API functionality)
- **PUT /reviews/:reviewId** - Update a review (adapt to use existing Review API functionality)
- **PATCH /reviews/:reviewId** - Partially update a review (adapt to use existing Review API functionality)
- **DELETE /reviews/:reviewId** - Delete a review (adapt to use existing Review API functionality)

#### ReviewType Endpoints

- **POST /reviewTypes** - Create a new review type (evaluate if this can use existing Review API functionality)
- **GET /reviewTypes** - List review types (evaluate if this can use existing Review API functionality)
- **GET /reviewTypes/:reviewTypeId** - Get a specific review type (evaluate if this can use existing Review API functionality)
- **PUT /reviewTypes/:reviewTypeId** - Update a review type (evaluate if this can use existing Review API functionality)
- **PATCH /reviewTypes/:reviewTypeId** - Partially update a review type (evaluate if this can use existing Review API functionality)
- **DELETE /reviewTypes/:reviewTypeId** - Delete a review type (evaluate if this can use existing Review API functionality)

#### ReviewSummation Endpoints

-  **POST /reviewSummations** - Create a new review summation
-  **GET /reviewSummations** - List review summations
- **GET /reviewSummations/:reviewSummationId** - Get a specific review summation
- **PUT /reviewSummations/:reviewSummationId** - Update a review summation
- **PATCH /reviewSummations/:reviewSummationId** - Partially update a review summation
- **DELETE /reviewSummations/:reviewSummationId** - Delete a review summation

### 3. New Functionality

Implement the new functionality as specified in the requirements:

- **GET /submissions/download/:challengeId** - Download all submissions for a challenge as a ZIP file

## Submission Guidelines

Please submit:

1. A git patch against the develop branch of [https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api.git](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api.git), which should include:
   - Updated Prisma schema with new models
   - NestJS controllers, services, and DTOs for all endpoints
   - OpenAPI documentation for all endpoints

2. A design document (in markdown format) explaining:
   - The integration approach
   - Mapping between old and new data models
   - Authentication and authorization strategy
   - Any deviations from the original API (if necessary)

3. A deployment guide (in markdown format).

## Final Notes

- Ask questions in the challenge forum for clarifications. Do not make assumptions without approval.
- Use the given [GitHub repo's develop branch](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api) as the code base.
- Ensure Swagger docs are accessible at http://localhost:3000/api-docs.
- The goal is to have a seamless transition from the old Submissions API to the new Review API without requiring changes to dependent services.

name: [72hr] TopCoder Member API: Prisma Schema Update
description:
## Project Overview

The current Member API works and solves a lot of problems we had with syncing data between Informix, Postgres,
Dynamo, and ElasticSearch, but at the cost of code and architectural complexity and the syncing isn't exactly right all the time. 
Also it is difficult to run locally as there are a number of dependencies, and running it also involves
potentially having to have VPN access to a Topcoder AWS account, which isn't possible if we ever wanted to work on it
with members.

So, we are aiming to revise the Member API to reduce the complexity on the dependencies.
We need to **remove dependencies on Informix, Dynamo, and ElasticSearch** and **use Postgres with Prisma ORM** as the sole backend database.
After getting the Prisma schema created, we should remove the DAL / gRPC dependencies completely and update the existing code to just use prisma instead of saving/retrieving from ES/OS.

This challenge focuses on updating the database schema (using Prisma ORM) and focus on the JSON field re-design.

## Requirements
### Prisma Schema Update
All the JSON fields are in scope of this challenge:
- model member: maxRating, addresses, aggregatedSkills and enteredSkills
- model distributionStats: distribution
- model memberHistoryStats: DEVELOP and DATA_SCIENCE
- model memberStats: maxRating, DEVELOP, DESIGN, DATA_SCIENCE and COPILOT
- model memberTrait: traits

#### traits
We have a formal set of traits we support:
- Devices (see https://account-settings.topcoder.com/#tools)
- Software (see https://account-settings.topcoder.com/#tools)
- Work (see Experience on profiles.topcoder.com/phead)
- Education (see Education and Certifications on profiles.topcoder.com/phead)
- Onboarding Checklist (see response from `v5/members/phead/traits?` on https://account-settings.topcoder.com/#tools)
- Basic info (see response from `v5/members/phead/traits?` on https://account-settings.topcoder.com/#tools)
- Languages (Set on profiles.topcoder.com/phead)
- Communities (Set on https://account-settings.topcoder.com/#tcandyou)
- Personalization (currently just the user consent flag on https://account-settings.topcoder.com/#account)

Let's define sub-objects / tables for each trait, instead of just using JSON.  If we ever want to add new traits, we'll update the schema with a new trait table, instead of storing JSON.

These categories are deprecated:
- Connect User Information

#### maxRating, DEVELOP, DESIGN, DATA_SCIENCE and COPILOT
- refer to the link: https://profiles.topcoder.com/phead/stats/Development
![image.png](https://cdn.filestackcontent.com/lX3MwSHT16t3oOsXSuDR)

#### distribution
- refer to the link: https://profiles.topcoder.com/phead/stats/Development/CODE
![image.png](https://cdn.filestackcontent.com/D5rcaxSRTRuhGKcyLz4j)

#### addresses
- refer to the link: https://profiles.topcoder.com/{your_id}

#### aggregatedSkills and enteredSkills
- Let's keep these two as JSON type for now.

## Technology Stack

- Backend: NodeJS
- ORM: Prisma (with PostgreSQL)
- API Docs: OpenAPI/Swagger

## Resources
- Member API code: [https://github.com/topcoder-platform/member-api/tree/develop](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/member-api/tree/develop
- Prisma: [https://www.prisma.io/docs/orm/overview/introduction/what-is-prisma](https://www.prisma.io/docs/orm/overview/introduction/what-is-prisma)
- Sample Prisma Schema: [https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api/blob/develop/prisma/schema.prisma](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api/blob/develop/prisma/schema.prisma)

## Submission Guidelines

- updated Prisma schema file.
- updated Swagger file.
- A readme file explaining the transformation from Informix/ES/Dynamo to Prisma schema and verification.

## Judging Criteria:

- Completeness of all transformations from Informix/ES/Dynamo to Prisma schema
- Clarity and accuracy of OpenAPI documentation.
- Adherence to Prisma best practices (e.g., indexes, naming).

## Final Notes

- Ask questions in the challenge forum for clarifications, Do not make assumptions without approval.

name: Topcoder Identity Service: updating AuthorizationResource with TypeScript and Postgres
description:
## Project Overview
The Identity Service project is used  to validate username / password logins, lookup roles, etc...  It's currently implemented in Java and targets MySQL/Informix.
We want to revise the project to use **TypeScript** and point to **Postgres** via **Prisma**, but **keep the functionalities untouched, such as redis cache, Kafka event bus, etc**.  

In previous challenges: [Topcoder Identity Service: revise using Typescript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/6cf9672f-6a55-4043-8661-df9917bb3271),  [Topcoder Identity Service: updating User Resources with TypeScript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/6ab232e5-16c4-4e81-a909-827ac897bbdf) and [Topcoder Identity Service: updating GroupResource with TypeScript and Postgres [Repost]
](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/b1fd57aa-5616-4c0e-8b08-683c1b1981fb), we'v setup the project structure with TypeScript and Postgres, and migrated **RoleResource**, **UserResource** and **GroupResource**.
In this challenge, we're aiming to migrate  resource  **AuthorizationResource** and fix several issues in previous challenge.

## Detail Requirements
- The endpoints need to be considered to migrate is **AuthorizationResource**;
-  Keep features in the Identity Service, such as **redis** as cache, **Kafka** message sending through the event bus, **Shiro-like** permission check ;
- Make sure to use two data sources: one for tables currently exist in **common_oltp** database  from **Postgres**, another for the rest of the data in **MySQL**.
- Make sure that the migration can happen and people can still log in. We should migrate the passwords without having to do anything - they are encrypted in the DB and currently what are used for authentication;
- Write unit tests for these endpoints in AuthorizationResource and make sure **test coverage >= 90%**%;
- Write or update postman test collection for these endpoints in AuthorizationResource;
- Update Swagger file properly for each apis in AuthorizationResource;
- Fix the following issues from previous code:
	- **Remove all Dice related code and text since we are deprecating Dice ID as the 2FA mechanism for Topcoder login, we are instead going to replace it with standard Auth0 2FA, like a normal code generator.**
	- define a constant like following to replace all the specified constants in the code(group.controller.ts, group.service.ts):				
![image.png](https://cdn.filestackcontent.com/i8bgSVlfRGyn6BRiXV2f)
	- validateMembership should be handled properly. In original code, validateMembership validates groupMembership.getMembershipTypeId is null or not, but in the revised code, it’s checking groupMembership.getMembershipType which is different.
- Update **README** file on how to set up instruction, how to test, how to verify;

## Technology Stack

- NodeJS
- Java
- MySQL
- Postgres version **16.3**
- ORM: Prisma (with PostgreSQL)

## Resources
- Exist Identity Service:  https://github.com/appirio-tech/identity-service/tree/dev
- Revised Identity Service: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/identity-api-v6/tree/develop
- Data in MySQL&Postgres: Will provide in forum
- Dependencies for Exist Identity Service: Will provide in forum

## Submission Guidelines

✅ Source code
✅ README.md: Setup instructions, environment variables, and verification details.

## Final Notes

- Ask questions in the challenge forum for clarifications, Do not make assumptions without approval.
- Maintain clean, structured, and well-documented code .

name: Topcoder Marathon Match Scorer Rebuild POC Part 2 Relaunched
description:
### Project overview
Currently, We have a single large EC2 instance that runs all the time. The EC2 instance runs a simple processor that handles Kafka messages to a few queues,
When a submission comes in, the Kafka message is sent by the submission API and the processor picks up docker process to score the submission back to submission API. 

Docker hub isn't ideal for our platform purposes.  It would be much better if the compiled scorer lived in ECR, which is where all our production code is sent and then deployed via Fargate / ECS.

We'd like to get away from an always-running EC2 instance for the scorer since that's expensive and unnecessary when it's just not scoring or doing anything (like right now between MMs).  Instead, scoring will be on-demand for each submission coming in.

We have done the first challenge of this series which identified the new workflow with fake scorer. We'd like to explore the implementation of scorer in this challenge.

### Individual Requirements
In this challenge, we need to replace the docker scorer to Fargate scorer. We may need move the common code into the Fargate scorer implementation so that we can change the configuration and actual tester class/code when do the testing for each challenge.

**Notes: LET"S ONLY FOCUS ON THE JAVA SCORER.**

#### Scorer Configuration
1. Configuration or environment variables could be moved to Parameter Store. Check com.topcoder.marathon.Parameters for reference. We can change the configuration between the challenges easily without reach out the code.
1. We'll need a way to configure the scorer to use for each challenge ID.  Note that the current Docker scorer (tc-mm-processor) has a configuration value for the challenge ID to "watch" and process submissions for.
```
 // Check for expected challengeId
    if (!challengeId || challengeId !== targetedChallenge) {
      logger.info(
        `Ignoring message as challengeId - ${challengeId} is not of interest`
      )58=55202
      return
    }
```
We'll want a way to do multiple challenges and multiple scorers, with the potential for multiple scorers to run for a single challenge ID as well.  For instance, on MM 161, we have example scoring and provisional scoring for each submission, which is two separate EC2 instances (one running execute.sh on each submission and one running executeExample.sh on each submission).  Currently, in addition to the different scorer / review types, if we have concurrent MMs running, we just run a whole separate EC2 instance for each challenge, where each instance is dedicated to a single scorer, which isn't efficient.

3. Refer to https://docs.aws.amazon.com/systems-manager/latest/userguide/systems-manager-parameter-store.html about how to read values from Parameter Store.

#### The structure of the scorer
I have attached a completed scorer tc-mm-160. Basically, there are two part of the scorer:
1. marathon, ReviewHelper and ReviewTest are almost same between different challenges.
2. BioSlimeTester.java: this is the test code for the specific challenge. 

#### The output of the scorer
* In current implementation of scorer, the score of the submission reads from file (L157 - L162 in KafkaHandlerService.js), we should design a way to send back to main lambda function.
* After receive the score, the main lambda function should create a review through review api.

#### The Submission Lifecycle
We'd like to introduce following status to the submissions:
1. submitted
2. running examples
3. running provisional
4. completed

So when the submissions coming, we need to mark the status to submitted, and then run the examples and provisional scorers. When the process finished, it need to mark the status to completed.

### Submission
Your submission should include:

* A zip file contains all updated codes.
* A comprehensive README that covers the deployment and configuration of the code (if any configuration changes were required)
* A validation markdown file that covers how you tested the new scorer functionality

name: Topcoder NestJS Autopilot: Event-Based Phase Transition Implementation
description:
The Topcoder Autopilot service is responsible for automatically transitioning challenges through their phases at the appropriate times. In [the first challenge](http://www.topcoder.com/challenge-details/30376827/?type=develop), we established the core project infrastructure and integrated with Kafka for event messaging. This second challenge focuses on implementing an event-based mechanism to replace the current polling approach, making the system more efficient and responsive.

### Technology Stack

- NestJS 10+
- TypeScript 5+
- @nestjs/schedule module
- Kafka
- ESLint and Prettier for code formatting
- Docker

### Challenge Background

The current Autopilot-v6 codebase has established the Kafka infrastructure for sending and receiving events but lacks the core scheduling functionality. While the system can manually trigger events, there's no automated scheduling mechanism to trigger them at specific phase end times. This challenge aims to implement this functionality using NestJS's scheduling capabilities.

### Codebase

- [https://github.com/topcoder-platform/autopilot-v6.git](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/autopilot-v6.git)

### Individual Requirements

#### 1. Event-Based Scheduling Mechanism

Implement a dynamic scheduling system using NestJS's @nestjs/schedule module:

- Add the @nestjs/schedule module to the project dependencies
- Create a scheduler service that leverages SchedulerRegistry to manage dynamic job registration and cancellation
- Implement methods to schedule one-time tasks based on phase end times
- Each scheduled job should correspond to a specific phase end event

#### 2. Event Generation

Enhance the existing Kafka infrastructure to automatically generate events:

- At the scheduled time, trigger phase transition events
- Publish these events to the appropriate Kafka topic (autopilot.phase.transition)
- Ensure the event payload includes challenge ID, phase ID, and other relevant metadata as per the existing message format

#### 3. Schedule Adjustment Handling

Implement logic to handle changes to phase schedules:

- Create handlers for challenge/phase updates that might affect scheduled end times
- Implement logic to cancel existing scheduled jobs when phase end times are changed
- Re-schedule events with updated timing information
- Ensure all schedule changes are properly logged for audit purposes

#### 4. Resilience and Recovery

Implement mechanisms to ensure the system can recover from failures:

- Create a recovery mechanism that executes when the service restarts
- On startup, scan all active phases and schedule events for upcoming transitions
- Handle edge cases like immediate phase transitions (end times in the past)
- Implement proper error handling and logging throughout the scheduling process

#### 5. Documentation

- Document the scheduling approach and recovery mechanisms
- Include examples of how the system handles different scenarios (normal scheduling, rescheduling, recovery)

### Implementation Details

#### Scheduler Service

Create a new service that will:

1. Use the SchedulerRegistry from @nestjs/schedule to manage dynamic jobs
2. Provide methods to:
   - Schedule a phase transition event
   - Cancel a scheduled phase transition
   - Update a scheduled phase transition
   - Get all scheduled transitions

Example interface (Just for your reference):

```typescript
interface SchedulerService {
  schedulePhaseTransition(phaseData: PhaseTransitionScheduleDto): Promise<string>; // returns job id
  cancelScheduledTransition(jobId: string): Promise<boolean>;
  updateScheduledTransition(jobId: string, phaseData: PhaseTransitionScheduleDto): Promise<string>; // returns new job id
  getAllScheduledTransitions(): Promise<ScheduledTransitionInfo[]>;
}
```

#### Phase Transition Handler

Enhance the existing AutopilotService to:

1. Process phase transition requests
2. Schedule phase transitions based on end times
3. Handle updates to phase schedules
4. Trigger the appropriate Kafka events when transitions occur

#### Recovery Mechanism

Implement a recovery service that:

1. Runs on application startup
2. Fetches all active phases from the challenge service
3. Schedules transitions for phases that haven't ended yet
4. Immediately processes phases that should have already ended

### Important Notes

1. The existing Kafka infrastructure should be leveraged for event publishing
2. All scheduled jobs should be properly managed to prevent memory leaks
3. The solution should be designed with scalability in mind, potentially allowing for distributed scheduling in the future
4. Proper error handling and logging are essential for debugging and monitoring 
5. All required features are properly implemented
6. Ask questions in the challenge forum for clarifications, Do not make assumptions without approval.

### Submission Guideline

Please submit a git patch against the latest commit in the develop branch of [https://github.com/topcoder-platform/autopilot-v6.git](https://github.com/topcoder-platform/autopilot-v6.git), which should include

1. Complete implementation of the event-based scheduling mechanism
2. Integration with the existing Kafka infrastructure
3. Documentation on the scheduling approach and recovery mechanisms
4. The deployment guide (in markdown format).

description:

name: Topcoder Resource API - add support for memberId for resource API
description:
## Project Overview

The current challenge API works and solves a lot of problems we had with syncing data between Informix, Postgres,
Dynamo, and ElasticSearch, but at the cost of code and architectural complexity and the syncing isn't exactly right all the time. 
Also it is difficult to run locally as there are a number of dependencies, and running it also involves
potentially having to have VPN access to a Topcoder AWS account, which isn't possible if we ever wanted to work on it
with members.

So, we are aiming to revise the **resources API** to reduce the complexity on the dependencies.
We need to **remove dependencies on Informix, Dynamo, and ElasticSearch** and **use Postgres with Prisma ORM** as the sole backend database.
After getting the Prisma schema created, we should remove the DAL / gRPC dependencies completely and update the existing code to just use prisma instead of saving/retrieving from ES/OS.

In order to achieve the above goal, we have updated the code in challenge [Topcoder Resources API: Update Code for Prisma Schema and updated Swagger
](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/77910b35-7ad7-4e67-a953-85d88b7d6cf6) and have written Data migration tools in challenge [Topcoder Resources API: Data Migration Tool From Dynamo to Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/f0cc9d2f-e2c4-48f6-9381-ccfb92330773)

The goal for this F2F challenge is to add support for memberId in resource API.

# Individual Requirements
- Currently the resource API uses `memberHandle`, which can be changed,  to identify a user. Thus, we need to add `memberId` as a new approach to identify a user. In the meantime, we still need to support `memberHandle` as existing code still uses it.
There are two specified APIs involved shown as below (If new API is found, it's also in scope.):
https://github.com/topcoder-platform/resource-api-v6/blob/6ed0e24f07099f1e69ae9cc2baadf272edeedddd/src/services/ResourceService.js#L369
https://github.com/topcoder-platform/resource-api-v6/blob/6ed0e24f07099f1e69ae9cc2baadf272edeedddd/src/services/ResourceService.js#L452
- make sure `memberHandle` or `memberId` is passed in the request body. Make sure to use `memberid` firstly, then  `memberHandle`. 
- Update all APIs to set `createdBy` and `updatedBy` as `memberId`, not `memberHandle`.
- Fix the tests if they're failed due to changes in code;
# Resources
- Resource API code repo: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/resource-api-v6/tree/develop

A patch file against the develop branch of resource API
A comprehensive README that covers the deployment and configuration of the code (if any configuration changes were required)
A validation markdown file that covers how you tested the functionality

name: Topcoder Review API - Implement Configurable PostgreSQL Schema
description:
The Topcoder Review API currently uses the default public schema in PostgreSQL. In production and development environments, we need to use different schema names for better organization and security. This First2Finish challenge requires implementing support for configurable PostgreSQL schema names throughout the application.

### Technology Stack

- Backend: NestJS
- ORM: Prisma (with PostgreSQL)
- ID Format: nanoID
- API Docs: OpenAPI/Swagger
- Package Manager: pnpm

### Required Links

- GitHub Repo: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api

### Core Requirements:

1. **Connection String Configuration**  

   Implement schema configuration via the DATABASE_URL connection string:
   - Remove the schema parameter from the DATABASE_URL (e.g., **postgresql://user:password@localhost:5432/mydb**)
   - Create an environment variable **POSTGRES_SCHEMA** for the schema configuration.
   - Ensure the application can read and use this parameter correctly
   - Implement a solution that allows for easy schema configuration in different environments

2. **Environment Variable Support**

   Provide flexible environment variable configuration:
   - Ensure the existing **POSTGRES_SCHEMA** environment variable is properly used if present, if the **POSTGRES_SCHEMA** is not provided, fallback to **public** schema
   - Update **.env.sample** with appropriate documentation for schema configuration options

3. **Migration Support**

   Update the migration process to support custom schema:
   - Ensure migrations run correctly on the configured schema
   - Update any custom migration scripts (including **migrate.ts**) to respect the configured schema
   - Provide clear documentation on how to run migrations with a custom schema

4. **Documentation Updates**

   Update documentation to reflect schema configuration:
   - Update README.md with instructions on schema configuration
   - Document the schema configuration process for development and deployment
   - Provide examples for common deployment scenarios

### Acceptance Criteria

1. The application must support configuring PostgreSQL schema via the DATABASE_URL and POSTGRES_SCHEMA environment variables
2. All migrations must run correctly on the configured schema
3. The application must work correctly with custom schema names
4. Documentation must be updated with clear instructions for schema configuration
5. No regression in existing functionality

### Submission Guidelines

Please submit a git patch against the latest commit in the **feat/review-opportunities** branch of https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api.git, which should include:

1. Updated code with schema configuration support (if needed)
2. Updated migration scripts (if needed)
3. Updated documentation with clear instructions
4. Any necessary tests to verify functionality

### Additional Notes

- The solution should be as simple as possible while meeting all requirements
- Prefer configuration over code changes when possible

name: Topcoder Review API - Fix Identified Issues
description:
The Topcoder Review API has several issues that need to be fixed to improve code quality, security, and functionality. This First2Finish challenge requires you to address the identified issues in the codebase, which include typos, security concerns, data type inconsistencies, and logical errors.

### Technology Stack

- Backend: NestJS
- ORM: Prisma (with PostgreSQL)
- ID Format: nanoID
- API Docs: OpenAPI/Swagger
- Package Manager: pnpm

### Required Links

- GitHub Repo: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api
- Issues found in GitHub review: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api/pull/6

### Core Requirements:

Fix the following identified issues

1. .env.sample

```
M2M_AUTH_DOMAIN="topcoder-dev.auth0.com"
M2M_AUTH_AUDIENCE="https://m2m.topcoder-dev.com/"
M2M_AUTH_PROXY_SEREVR_URL=
```


The variable M2M_AUTH_PROXY_SEREVR_URL seems to have a typo in its name. It should likely be M2M_AUTH_PROXY_SERVER_URL.

2. mock/mock-api.js

```
  res.json({})
})

const m2mToken = 'xxxx';
```

Storing sensitive information such as tokens directly in the code is not secure. Consider using environment variables or a secure vault to manage sensitive data.

We can't put these into any code, even if they are long since expired. Let's remove this and make it an env variable.

3. prisma/migrations/20250525042446_add_review_application/migration.sql

```
    "id" VARCHAR(14) NOT NULL DEFAULT nanoid(),
    "userId" TEXT NOT NULL,
    "handle" TEXT NOT NULL,
    "opportunityId" TEXT NOT NULL,
```

The column opportunityId is defined as TEXT NOT NULL, but it references the id column in the reviewOpportunity table, which is defined as VARCHAR(14). Consider using the same data type (VARCHAR(14)) for consistency and to avoid potential issues with data integrity.

4. prisma/migrations/20250525042446_add_review_application/migration.sql

```
    "incrementalPayment" DOUBLE PRECISION NOT NULL,
    "createdAt" TIMESTAMP(3) NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    "createdBy" TEXT NOT NULL,
    "updatedAt" TIMESTAMP(3) NOT NULL,
```

Consider adding a default value for the updatedAt column to ensure it is automatically set when a new record is created.

5. src/api/review-history/reviewHistory.controller.ts

```
  })
  @ApiResponse({ status: 400, description: 'Bad Request' })
  @ApiResponse({ status: 401, description: 'Unauthorized' })
  @ApiResponse({ status: 403, description: 'Unauthorized' })
```

The description for the 403 status code should be 'Forbidden' instead of 'Unauthorized' to accurately reflect the nature of the error.

6. src/api/review-opportunity/reviewOpportunity.controller.ts

```
    required: false
  })
  @ApiQuery({
    name: 'limit',
```

The query parameter name 'limit' is duplicated. Consider renaming the second 'limit' parameter to 'offset' to avoid confusion and ensure correct functionality.

7. src/api/review-opportunity/reviewOpportunity.service.ts

```
    }
    // sort list
    responseList = [...responseList].sort((a, b) => {
      return dto.sortOrder === 'asc' ? (a[dto.sortBy] - b[dto.sortBy]) : (a[dto.sortBy] - b[dto.sortBy]);
```

The sorting logic is incorrect. The comparison function should return a positive number, zero, or a negative number, but currently, it always returns zero because the same expression is used for both 'asc' and 'desc'. Consider using (a[dto.sortBy] - b[dto.sortBy]) for 'asc' and (b[dto.sortBy] - a[dto.sortBy]) for 'desc'.

8. src/dto/common.dto.ts

```
): ResponseDto<string> {
  const ret = new ResponseDto<string>();
  const result = new ResultDto<string>();
  result.success = true;
```

The success property is set to true in FailResponse, which seems incorrect for an error response. Consider setting it to false to accurately represent a failure.

9. src/dto/reviewApplication.dto.ts

```
  @ApiProperty({
    description: 'Review Application Role',
  })
  role: string;
```

Consider using the ReviewApplicationRole type for the role property instead of string to ensure type safety and consistency with the CreateReviewApplicationDto class.

10. src/dto/reviewOpportunity.dto.ts

```
    example: '50.0',
  })
  @IsNumber()
  incrementalPayment: number;
```

Consider adding @IsPositive() to ensure incrementalPayment is a positive number, similar to basePayment.

11. src/dto/reviewOpportunity.dto.ts

```
      'Review payment. Should be base payment if there is 1 submission.',
    example: 180.0,
  })
  payment: number;
```

Consider adding validation decorators such as @IsNumber() and @IsPositive() to ensure payment is a positive number.

12. src/shared/config/m2m.config.ts

```
    url: process.env.M2M_AUTH_URL ?? 'http://localhost:4000/oauth/token',
    domain: process.env.M2M_AUTH_DOMAIN ?? 'topcoder-dev.auth0.com',
    audience: process.env.M2M_AUTH_AUDIENCE ?? 'https://m2m.topcoder-dev.com/',
    proxyUrl: process.env.M2M_AUTH_PROXY_SEREVR_URL,
```

Typo in the environment variable name: M2M_AUTH_PROXY_SEREVR_URL should be M2M_AUTH_PROXY_SERVER_URL.


### Submission Guidelines

Please submit a git patch against the latest commit in the **feat/review-opportunities** branch of https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api.git, which should include:

1. All required fixes implemented
2. Updated code comments where necessary
3. No regression in existing functionality
4. No new issues introduced

### Verification Process

Your submission will be verified by:
1. Checking that all identified issues are fixed
2. Running the application to ensure no regressions
3. Code review to ensure quality and adherence to project standards

name: Topcoder Identity Service: features improvement and issues fix
description:
## Project Overview
The Identity Service project is used  to validate username / password logins, lookup roles, etc...  It's currently implemented in Java and targets MySQL/Informix.
We want to revise the project to use **TypeScript** and point to **Postgres** via **Prisma**, but **keep the functionalities untouched, such as redis cache, Kafka event bus, etc**.  

In previous challenges: [Topcoder Identity Service: revise using Typescript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/6cf9672f-6a55-4043-8661-df9917bb3271),  [Topcoder Identity Service: updating User Resources with TypeScript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/6ab232e5-16c4-4e81-a909-827ac897bbdf) , [Topcoder Identity Service: updating GroupResource with TypeScript and Postgres [Repost]](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/b1fd57aa-5616-4c0e-8b08-683c1b1981fb) and [Topcoder Identity Service: updating AuthorizationResource with TypeScript and Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/a4f17d0f-2196-4d00-8c29-3a33871da825), we'v setup the project structure with TypeScript and Postgres, and migrated **RoleResource**, **UserResource**, **GroupResource** and **AuthorizationResource**.
In this challenge, we're aiming to do some feature improvement and fix several issues in previous challenge.

## Detail Requirements

- The current identity service is you are able to create multiple roles with the same name.  Make sure that roles have a unique name enforced. 
- Clean up the schema to only the tables / models that are used in the code.
- Make sure all the Controllers have API docs. You can refer to group.controller.ts#L61-69 for example.
- Replace all the hard coded status numbers in controllers and services with specified constants.  You can refer to group.controller.ts#L67 for example. 
- Add comments to each api methods in each controller. Currently, the comments are presented in old identity service, but the new identity service does not have them.
- Define a constant class to replace all the specified constants in the code.
- Improve the code by extracting the common codes if appliable, i.e., group.controller.ts#L74-76, #209-211.
- Fix the following issues:
	- validateMembership should be handled properly. In original code, validateMembership validates groupMembership.getMembershipTypeId is null or not, but in the revised code, it’s checking groupMembership.getMembershipType which is different.
	- Fix the npm run lint error
	- Fix the hard coded issue in the code: 
		1. Target ID hardcoded as '1' (Multiple locations)
				auth.target = '1';
				await this.service.deleteObject('1', req, res);
				Multiple test files: Using '1' as default target ID
		2. Default redirect URL hardcoded
				redirectUrl = "https://www.topcoder.com";
		3. Random string length hardcoded
			this.randomString(12) - Magic number 12 for state generation
		4. Subject type hardcoded
			subjectType: 1 - Magic number for role assignment queries
		5. Auth0 prefix hardcoded
			sub.substring('auth0|'.length) - Hardcoded Auth0 prefix
		6. Hardcode value: L517, L535, L461, L191, L181 in AuthorizationService

- Improve unit tests for all endpoints and make sure **test coverage >= 90%**%;
- Write or update postman test collection for all endpoints;
- Update **README** file on how to set up instruction, how to test, how to verify;

## Technology Stack

- NodeJS
- Java
- MySQL
- Postgres version **16.3**
- ORM: Prisma (with PostgreSQL)

## Resources
- Exist Identity Service:  https://github.com/appirio-tech/identity-service/tree/dev
- Revised Identity Service: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/identity-api-v6/tree/develop
- Data in MySQL&Postgres: Will provide in forum
- Dependencies for Exist Identity Service: Will provide in forum

## Submission Guidelines

✅ Source code
✅ README.md: Setup instructions, environment variables, and verification details.

## Final Notes

- Ask questions in the challenge forum for clarifications, Do not make assumptions without approval.
- Maintain clean, structured, and well-documented code .

name: Topcoder Platform - V6 Groups API implementation
description:
This challenge starts the process of migrating our existing groups API from Javascript / Neo4j to Typescript / Postgres / NestJS to match our other new v6 APIS.  Currently, the groups API is the only service on the Topcoder platform using Neo4j, which makes it overly complex to manage and support.

### Technology Stack
* Backend: NestJS
* ORM: Prisma with PostgreSQL
* ID Format: UUID, to maintain existing ID structure
* API Documentation: OpenAPI/Swagger
* Package Manager: pnpm

### Required Links
GitHub Repo: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/groups-api

### Core Requirements:

The goal is to build a new groups service, in Typescript using NestJS.  The persistence layer will be Prisma target Postgres.

The functionality and endpoints will be **exactly** the same as they are in the current version of the Groups API, linked above.  You can find the existing Swagger here:  https://github.com/topcoder-platform/groups-api/blob/develop/docs/swagger.yml 

You can see available routes here:  https://github.com/topcoder-platform/groups-api/blob/051f8abcc7cf4a6fe7160c8b12ad9584777b274c/src/routes.js#L7-L8

#### Schema

You must design the Prisma schema to match the existing functionality for groups.  A "group" in the current API looks like this, and we should follow the same, with the exact same fields and formats. 

* "createdBy" will be a user ID, as a string
* "id" will be a GUID
* "status" can be one of "active" or "inactive"
* "domain" can be a string
* "selfRegister" is a boolean

```
    {
        "ssoId": "",
        "createdAt": "2017-11-06T10:29:38.000Z",
        "selfRegister": false,
        "createdBy": "8547899",
        "domain": "",
        "name": "Hide Challenges",
        "description": "Hide Challenges",
        "privateGroup": true,
        "id": "f325869a-ab0d-47b1-af28-4e117709ca71",
        "oldId": "20000014",
        "status": "active"
    },
    {
        "ssoId": "",
        "updatedBy": "00000000",
        "description": "Veterans",
        "privateGroup": true,
        "oldId": "20000013",
        "organizationId": "",
        "createdAt": "2017-10-19T21:15:51.000Z",
        "selfRegister": true,
        "createdBy": "8547899",
        "domain": "veterans",
        "name": "Veterans",
        "id": "53eddd4a-e554-48e9-8fd4-b57406008b76",
        "updatedAt": "2020-09-08T17:01:35.096Z",
        "status": "active"
    },
```

Group membership looks like the example below, pulled from an API call like: https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v5/groups/f325869a-ab0d-47b1-af28-4e117709ca71/members 

```

    {
        "id": "5c50ee2d-5a42-4130-97c3-5ceb743e6a69",
        "groupId": "f325869a-ab0d-47b1-af28-4e117709ca71",
        "groupName": "Hide Challenges",
        "createdAt": "2022-09-02T09:16:55.683Z",
        "createdBy": "40029484",
        "memberId": "23175096",
        "universalUID": "00000000",
        "membershipType": "user"
    }
```
Please ensure you provide scripts in your package.json to:

* Initialize the database using the Prisma schema
* Reset the database using the Prisma schema

### Pagination

The "perPage" and "page" parameters should work as expected for pagination, and the response headers should also include the "X-..." headers, as shown here:  https://github.com/topcoder-platform/groups-api/blob/051f8abcc7cf4a6fe7160c8b12ad9584777b274c/src/common/helper.js#L209-L210

### Authorization

For testing the roles in a JWT, you can follow what the new tc-review-api does:  https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api/blob/27c8160842d6b8cb312955ec11eb2ad43ee750a3/src/shared/modules/global/jwt.service.ts#L1-L2

For the M2M scopes that apply to each endpoint, follow what's in the existing code here:  https://github.com/topcoder-platform/groups-api/blob/051f8abcc7cf4a6fe7160c8b12ad9584777b274c/src/routes.js#L7-L8

A script to generate an M2M token with the groups scopes will be provided in the forum.

### Database

It's important to _not_ default the database to use the "public" schema.  In production, the database tables for groups will be in a "groups" schema.  We now support individual schemas for all our other Postgres services.

### Kafka

Publishing the Kafka messages to the configured topics should work the exact same.  You can target the bus API in the exact same manner as the existing code.  

The current code uses a wrapper found here:  https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-bus-api-wrapper

The tc-bus-api-wrapper should **NOT** be used in this implementation, but you **CAN** use the tc-core-library-js.  An example from the new tc-review-api is below.

* Getting an M2M token using the configured values
  * https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api/blob/27c8160842d6b8cb312955ec11eb2ad43ee750a3/src/shared/modules/global/m2m.service.ts#L1-L2
* Sending a request to the bus API: 
  * https://github.com/topcoder-platform/tc-review-api/blob/27c8160842d6b8cb312955ec11eb2ad43ee750a3/src/shared/modules/global/eventBus.service.ts#L1-L2

### OpenAPI Spec

All endpoints must be decorated using:

* Role annotations (like "@Roles('administrator')")
* NestJS Swagger decorators ("@ApiProperty", "@ApiResponse", etc...)

### Redis

The current codebase can use Redis as a cache.  We will not do that for our implementation.  We will always just go to the database for data.  We can implement the "/groups/flushCache" endpoint as a no-op for now, just returning a 200 HTTP response.

### Logging

Please ensure logging is implemented in a way that all errors are logged and general messages are logged in an informative manner which will be useful for debugging.  

### Validation

You can compare your local implementation against the existing API in dev hosted at "https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v5/groups..."

### Submission Guidelines
Please submit: 

* A new project containing the Typescript code implementing the requirements above.  You should ensure that you include the detailed Prisma schema.
* Deployment guide

### Final Notes
Ask questions in the challenge forum for clarifications. Do not make assumptions without approval.
Use the given GitHub repo's master branch for comparison.

Ensure Swagger docs are accessible at http://localhost:3000/api-docs.

### Upcoming

A future challenge will tackle migrating data from the existing Neo4j data store to the new database, using the API.  We will also add unit tests.

name: 72 Hours!! - Topcoder Review App - API Integration for My Active Challenge Page
description:
**Note: this is a fast challenge with shorter submission phase, review phase, you only need to integrate an API endpoint for a single page.** 


The Topcoder Review App currently uses mock data for displaying active challenges in the My Active Challenge Page. This challenge focuses on integrating real API functionality into this page by replacing the mock data with actual API calls to the Challenge API.

### Technology Stack

- Frontend:
  - React
  - TypeScript
  - SCSS + CSS Modules

### Assets

- Review App (platform-ui): https://github.com/topcoder-platform/platform-ui
- Challenge API (challenge-api-v6):
  - Source code: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/challenge-api-v6.git
  - Hosted version:  https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v6/challenges

### Requirements

#### 1. API Integration for My Active Challenge Page

##### 1.1 Frontend Update (platform-ui)

Update the ActiveReviewsPage component and related services to fetch data from the real Challenge API:

Implementation Requirements:
- Replace mock data in **fetchActiveReviews()** function with real API calls to the Challenge API endpoint: https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v6/challenges/?status=ACTIVE&memberId={memberId}

- Update the **useFetchActiveReviews()** hook to handle API response format
- Add appropriate error handling and loading states
- Preserve the existing filter functionality for challenge types
- Update the **ChallengeInfo** model to match the structure of the Challenge API response
- Transform the API response data to match the expected format for the UI components

Code Updates:
- Update **reviews.service.ts** to make real API calls instead of using mock data
- Remove references to mock data from **useFetchActiveReviews.ts**
- Update **TableActiveReviews** component to display data from the real API
- Add proper loading state indicators and error handling

##### 1.2 Authentication and Error Handling

Implement proper authentication and error handling:

Implementation Requirements:
- Create an auth service that:
  - Retrieves the current user's authentication token
  - Adds authorization headers to API requests
  - Gets the current user's memberId for filtering challenges
- Implement comprehensive error handling:
  - Add proper loading indicators for API calls in progress
  - Display user-friendly error messages
  - Handle empty data scenarios

##### 1.3 Data Transformation

Create utility functions to transform the Challenge API response to match the expected format:

Implementation Requirements:
- Create a transformation function that converts Challenge API response to the ChallengeInfo model
- Calculate derived fields like:
  - currentPhaseEndDateString
  - timeLeft
  - timeLeftColor
  - timeLeftStatus
  - reviewProgress
- Extract the user's role for each challenge from the API response
- Implement proper filtering based on challenge type

##### 1.4 API Service Architecture

Create a clean API service architecture in platform-ui:

Implementation Requirements:
- Create a base API service class with common functionality:
  - Token management
  - Error handling
  - Request/response logging
- Implement proper TypeScript interfaces for all API responses
- Add caching for API responses to improve performance

### Important Notes

1. The Challenge API endpoint https://api.topcoder-dev.com/v6/challenges/?status=ACTIVE&memberId={memberId} will return only challenges where the current user is a resource.

2. The memberId parameter should be the ID of the currently logged-in user.

3. Remove related mock data and references after implementing real API integration. Update imports and dependencies accordingly.

4. Make sure to implement proper loading states and error handling for all API integrations.

5. All API calls should be properly typed using TypeScript interfaces.

6. Implement appropriate caching strategies for API responses to improve performance and reduce API calls.

7. Challenge API (challenge-api-v6) contains all the endpoints needed for this integration. No modifications to these services are required.

8. Ask questions in the challenge forum for clarifications, Do not make assumptions without approval.

### Submission Guidelines

Your submission should include:

1. Git patches against the specified branch in the repository:
   - platform-ui: the feat/review branch of https://github.com/topcoder-platform/platform-ui 

2. Documentation (in markdown) explaining:
   - How the Challenge API is integrated into the My Active Challenge Page
   - How the data transformation is implemented
   - How to test the integration

name: Topcoder Challenge API - Add field for "challengeSource"
description:
### Project overview

The current challenge  API works and solves a lot of problems we had with syncing data between Informix, Postgres,
Dynamo, and ElasticSearch, but at the cost of code and architectural complexity and the syncing isn't exactly right all the time. 
Also it is difficult to run locally as there are a number of dependencies, and running it also involves
potentially having to have VPN access to a Topcoder AWS account, which isn't possible if we ever wanted to work on it
with members.

So, we are aiming to revise the challenge API to reduce the complexity on the dependencies.
We need to **remove dependencies on Informix, Dynamo, and ElasticSearch** and **use Postgres with Prisma ORM** as the sole backend database.
After getting the Prisma schema created, we should remove the DAL / gRPC dependencies completely and update the existing code to just use prisma instead of saving/retrieving from ES/OS.

In order to achieve the above goal, we have updated the code in challenge [Topcoder Challenge  API: Update Code for Prisma Schema and updated Swagger](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/7dc44b10-2e27-4e57-bec1-4891c34aec4a) and have written Data migration tools in challenge [Topcoder Challenge API: Data Migration Tool to Postgres](https://www.topcoder.com/challenges/22477223-2354-449d-a3b5-2d4d63c4cb25)

The goal for this challenge is to Add field for "challengeSource".

### Individual Requirements

We need a field to indicate the source of the challenge, like if it was launched from Work Manager, Topgear, Github, etc...

This would be an optional string text field (doesn't need to be an enum) at the root level of the challenge object. We can call it something like `challengeSource`.

Make sure this `challengeSource` is handled properly when creating/updating/searching/deleting challenge related APIs.

Please see: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/challenge-api-v6/issues/9

### Code

- Challenge API code repo: https://github.com/topcoder-platform/challenge-api-v6/tree/develop

### Unit tests

### Code quality
